questions.original_question: 2024年9月27日签署的股权转让协议完成之后，LTIC的股权结构是怎样的
questions.answer:
在2024年9月27日签署的股权转让协议完成后，LTIC的股权结构如下：Geely UK Limited持有LTIC 80%的股权，成为其主要股东。此次交易涉及800,000股LTIC股份，交易总额为10,880,000欧元。交易完成后，LTIC的剩余20%股权仍由Lotus Technology Innovative Limited持有。

以上信息基于2025年5月30日之前的知识。

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (the “LTIL”), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., recently entered into a share sale and transfer agreement (the “Share Transfer Agreement”) with Geely UK Limited (“Geely”), pursuant to which LTIL agrees to sell, and Geely agrees to purchase, 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (“LTIC”), a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000 (the “Transaction”). The Transaction closed simultaneously with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. Upon completion of the Transaction, Geely beneficially owns 80% of LTIC’s share capital. A copy of the Share Transfer Agreement is included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the foregoing description of the Share Transfer Agreement is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto. Share Sale and Transfer Agreement, dated September 27, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Geely UK Limited.

rag_info.chunk_content: As consideration for Westwood’s irrevocable commitment to purchase ADSs upon the terms of and subject to satisfaction of the conditions set forth in the ELOC Purchase Agreement, Lotus Technology Corporation issued 700,000 Ordinary Shares to Westwood on September 19, 2024. In September 2024, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into a share sale and transfer agreement with Geely UK Limited, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Corporation sold 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH, or LTIC, a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Corporation, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000. Immediately upon completion of the transaction, Geely UK Limited beneficially owned 80% of LTIC’s share capital. On November 7, 2024, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into a bond subscription agreement, or the Bond Subscription Agreement, with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales, or the Subscriber, pursuant to which the Subscriber agrees to subscribe for and purchase, and Lotus Technology Corporation agrees to issue and sell to the Subscriber, a senior bond due 2029, or the 2029 Bond, with the principal amount of US$500,000,000, at a subscription price of 100% of the principal amount of the Bond. As of the date of this annual report, the transaction has not been closed yet.

rag_info.chunk_content: On December 2, 2021, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, a subsidiary of Lotus Technology, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at a consideration of US\$15.5 million, which was settled in June 2022. The transaction was accounted for as a common control transaction and completed in June 2022. On September 27, 2024, Lotus Technology disposed of 80% equity interest of Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH to Geely UK Limited, for cash consideration of US\$12.2 million. On May 13, 2022, Lotus Technology purchased a one-year convertible note with a principal amount of US\$10.0 million issued by ECARX Holding Inc. Upon the listing of ECARX Holding Inc. on December 21, 2022, the note was automatically converted into Class A ordinary shares of ECARX Holding Inc. at a conversion price of US\$9.50 per share. As of December 31, 2024 and 2023, the fair value of such shares was US\$2.2 million and US\$3.3 million, respectively. During the year ended December 31, 2024, Lotus Technology borrowed loans due within one year or less with principal amounts of US\$140.1 million from Zhejiang Geely Automobile Co., Ltd., and US\$58.4 million from Geely Technology Group Co., Ltd., respectively, bearing an interest rate of 6%. The loans are secured by Lotus Technology's intellectual property with a carrying amount of nil, as the research and development costs were expensed historically.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrent with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a put option agreement with each of Geely HK and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely HK and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely HK and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025 and contingent upon satisfaction of the LAT Put Option Exercise Condition. As of December 31, 2024, the Put Option Exercise Condition had been satisfied. On April 14, 2025, Geely HK exercised its put option, requiring LTC to purchase 51% of the equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd. Closing of the Geely HK put option could be subject to approvals of governmental authorities in various jurisdictions and is currently expected to take place around the end of 2025.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC entered into put option agreements with Geely HK and Etika, enabling them to require LTC to purchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK. The purchase price will be determined based on LGIL's total revenue for 2024, adjusted for net debt, and the options can be exercised between April 1 and June 30, 2025, provided certain conditions are met. By December 31, 2024, these conditions were fulfilled. Subsequently, on April 14, 2025, Geely HK exercised its option to sell 51% of its equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd. The completion of this transaction is expected to require governmental approvals and is projected to occur by the end of 2025.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Other Related Party Transactions 
summary: Lotus Tech has been actively involved in the sale of battery electric vehicles (BEVs), auto parts, and providing research and development (R&D) and consulting services, particularly to related parties. In the first half of 2023, the company reported a significant increase in service revenue, reaching $5.0 million, up from $1.9 million in the same period of 2022. Product sales also saw growth, with $807,000 in BEVs and auto parts sold to related parties in early 2023, compared to no sales in the previous year. By mid-2023, accounts receivable from related parties stood at $13.6 million, reflecting a consistent upward trend from previous years.

Lotus Tech's financial activities also include substantial transactions related to licensing agreements, notably with Zhejiang Liankong, a subsidiary of Geely Holding. Initially, the licensing cost was set at approximately $888.2 million but was later reduced to around $306.3 million. This license, which is limited to specific R&D projects, has been recorded as an expense, indicating no alternative future use. Additionally, the company has engaged in various financial arrangements with related parties, including R&D service payments, salary and expense reimbursements, and unsecured loans, which have contributed to a growing receivable balance.

The company has also entered into significant lease agreements for office and parking spaces, resulting in the recognition of right-of-use assets. In terms of purchases from related parties, Lotus Tech reported a dramatic increase to $264.4 million in the first half of 2023, compared to $10.1 million in the same period of 2022. This surge included substantial costs of goods sold and inventory levels.

Lotus Tech's financial obligations to related parties have increased over time, with amounts owed for various services and products rising significantly. The company has also engaged in equity transactions, including the acquisition of subsidiaries and investments in convertible notes, further expanding its financial landscape. Recent agreements, such as the distribution rights with Lotus Cars Limited and the trademark license agreements, highlight Lotus Tech's strategic partnerships and growth initiatives.

Overall, Lotus Tech's financial activities reflect a robust engagement with related parties, characterized by increasing revenues, significant investments in R&D, and a growing portfolio of agreements that position the company for future growth in the electric vehicle market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Background of the Business Combination 
summary: The Merger Agreement and related Transaction Documents between LTC and LCAA are the result of extensive negotiations following LCAA's establishment as a blank check company in January 2021 by affiliates of L Catterton. LCAA's primary goal is to facilitate business combinations, and it raised $294.7 million through its IPO in March 2021. Although LCAA evaluated around 80 potential acquisition targets, including seven specific companies, it ultimately focused on LTC for a proposed merger. 

The discussions between LCAA and LTC began in early 2022, with LCAA representatives conducting thorough due diligence on LTC's business and financial performance. Throughout the negotiation process, valuation discussions fluctuated due to market conditions, with LTC's enterprise value initially proposed at $12 billion but later adjusted to a range of $5.5 billion to $10 billion. 

On January 31, 2023, the parties finalized key agreements, including the Original Merger Agreement, which established the terms for the business combination. Subsequent amendments to the agreements were made, including provisions for PIPE financing and adjustments to the Sponsor Support Agreement. The merger aims to enhance strategic growth and operational efficiencies for both entities, with a focus on leveraging synergies to create shareholder value.

As of October 2023, the parties signed an amended agreement that revised the structure of the merger, ensuring that LCAA Public Shareholders would receive LTC American Depositary Shares instead of Ordinary Shares. Additionally, the Sponsor Support Agreement was amended to adjust the terms regarding the forfeiture and vesting of Sponsor Shares, reflecting the evolving nature of the partnership. Overall, the merger is positioned to capitalize on market opportunities and strengthen the combined entity's competitive positioning.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Cash flows and working capital 
summary: The cash flow summary for the years 2022 to 2024 provides a detailed analysis of the organization's financial movements, emphasizing operating, investing, and financing activities. In 2024, the organization reported a net cash outflow of $848,520 from operating activities, an increase from previous years, while investing activities also saw significant outflows totaling $579,440. However, financing activities generated a substantial net inflow of $1,459,527, reflecting the organization's reliance on various financing mechanisms, including bank loans and shareholder contributions.

The cash positions at the beginning of 2024 were $427,135, a decrease from prior years, but by the end of 2024, cash and restricted cash rose to $484,937. Exchange rate fluctuations positively impacted cash balances in 2024, contrasting with negative effects in the previous two years. Overall, the net cash increase for 2024 was $57,802, a turnaround from the decrease experienced in 2023.

Several significant financing agreements were established in 2024, including a $70 million convertible bond sale to Geely HK, a $500 million senior bond subscription with Kershaw Health Limited, and an exchangeable note agreement totaling RMB 600 million by Hangzhou Kingway Technology. The organization also engaged in a share sale agreement with Geely UK Limited and secured short-term loans backed by intellectual property.

Despite these financing efforts, the organization faces challenges related to profitability and cash flow management, raising concerns about its ability to sustain operations without effective strategies for securing additional funding. The company is exploring various options, including cutting expenses and pursuing new loans or equity financing, amidst uncertainties in global financial markets.

Additionally, the organization’s revenue is primarily denominated in RMB, which may complicate capital expenses and foreign currency conversions due to regulatory requirements in mainland China. The summary underscores the importance of effective cash management and strategic planning to navigate financial challenges and ensure continued operational viability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: A. History and Development of the Company 
summary: The Lotus BEV business, founded in 2018, operates through various entities in China, the UK, and Germany, including Wuhan Lotus Cars and Lotus Tech UK. A significant restructuring in 2023 eliminated the previous Variable Interest Entity (VIE) structure, streamlining operations under subsidiaries in mainland China. Key developments include the establishment of LTC in the Cayman Islands in 2021, the acquisition of equity interests in Lotus Tech UK and Lotus GmbH, and trademark licensing agreements with Group Lotus Limited. 

In March 2022, LTC executed a 10-for-1 stock split, increasing its ordinary shares significantly. The company continued its restructuring into 2023, moving away from the VIE model. In February 2024, LTC completed a business combination with LCAA, leading to the trading of its American Depositary Shares (ADSs) on Nasdaq. Subsequent financial activities included a private placement of convertible bonds with Geely HK and agreements for share purchases and bond subscriptions, indicating ongoing capital raising efforts. 

By September 2024, LTC had entered into agreements for significant share sales and bond subscriptions, including a $500 million bond agreement with Kershaw Health Limited. Future plans involve the acquisition of a controlling interest in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, which is crucial for manufacturing and consultancy services related to Lotus' sports cars and hypercars. The company’s principal executive office is located in Shanghai, China, with additional information available through its corporate website and the SEC's site.

title: Appendix II 
summary: Warranties serve as formal assurances from manufacturers or sellers regarding a product's quality, performance, and durability, promising that the item will function as advertised for a specified duration. They can include provisions for repairs, replacements, or refunds if the product fails to meet the promised standards. Warranties can be categorized into express warranties, which are explicitly stated, and implied warranties, which are assumed by law. It is crucial for consumers to comprehend the terms and conditions of warranties to fully understand their rights and the extent of coverage.

In a separate context, the transfer of equity interest involves the sale or transfer of ownership stakes in a company, typically represented by shares or stock. This process includes identifying the parties involved—the seller and the buyer—and specifying the type of equity being transferred, such as common or preferred stock. Key elements of the transfer include detailing the amount of equity, its valuation, and outlining the terms of the transfer, including payment methods and regulatory considerations. The impact on the company's ownership structure and any changes in control or influence are also essential factors to consider.

In a specific case, a 17.5% equity interest in the Target Company is being transferred from the Seller to the Buyer. The Seller has made a total capital contribution of RMB 12.25 million and holds the equity interest free from encumbrances. Upon closing, the Buyer will gain all associated rights, including dividends. The Seller confirms sole ownership and authority to execute the transfer, with no existing obligations that would affect the Target Company's registered capital.

The Target Company itself is a legally established limited liability entity under PRC laws, fully compliant with necessary qualifications and licenses for its operations. It is not engaged in significant legal disputes and has met all tax obligations and social insurance contributions. Financially, its Management Accounts reflect a sound position in accordance with accepted accounting standards, with no reported encumbrances or undisclosed liabilities. Overall, the Target Company is positioned to operate effectively without current adverse circumstances impacting its business.

The loans are secured by Lotus Technology's intellectual property with a carrying amount of nil, as the research and development costs were expensed historically.

summary: The company operates in the sale of sports cars, BEV lifestyle models, auto parts, and related products, while also offering R&D and consulting services. Revenue from these services increased from US$8.3 million in 2022 to US$24.4 million in 2024. Sales to related parties showed a significant rise, reaching US$18.2 million in 2024 from just US$23 thousand in 2022. Accounts receivable from related parties surged to US$107.8 million by the end of 2024, up from US$22.4 million in 2023, alongside contract liabilities of US$150 thousand for the same year.

The company also provided unsecured short-term loans to related parties, totaling US$0.6 million to US$2.3 million over the years from 2022 to 2024, with interest income recognized each year. Interest expenses related to delayed payments amounted to $17.5 million in 2024. The company also engaged in lease agreements, with current operating lease liabilities of $1.0 million as of December 31, 2024.

In terms of investments, the company completed an equity transfer agreement for $15.5 million in 2021 and later sold a portion of its interest back to Geely UK Limited for $12.2 million. Additionally, it purchased a convertible note from ECARX Holding Inc. in 2022, which converted into shares valued at $2.2 million by the end of 2024.

Overall, the company demonstrated robust growth in revenue and significant financial transactions with related parties, alongside increasing liabilities and strategic investments, reflecting its active engagement in the automotive sector and related services.

title: LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Lotus Technology Inc. Financial Activities and Developments**

Lotus Technology Inc. has undergone significant financial transactions and organizational changes over the past few years, culminating in a complex landscape of share issuances, mergers, and financial reporting adjustments.

1. **Share Issuances and Mergers**: The company issued a total of 8,427,821 ordinary shares, including 1,265,103 shares to public shareholders, 7,087,718 shares to the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP, and 75,000 shares to directors and officers. Notably, 20% of the Sponsor's shares remain unvested until specific conditions are met post-merger. Additionally, 36,597,038 shares were issued to settle WFOE Exchangeable Notes, and 3,011,368 shares were allocated for the conversion of Momenta Notes and Pre-IPO Notes. The company also issued 9,550,246 Public Warrants and 5,486,784 Sponsor Warrants, with a portion exercised in 2024.

2. **Financial Reporting and Restructuring**: Following the merger, Lotus is classified as the "acquired" company, with financial statements reflecting a continuation of operations. The net assets are recorded at historical cost, with no goodwill recognized. The company began trading on Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW" in February 2024.

3. **Reorganization and Control**: The company executed a series of reorganizations to consolidate control over its operations, including transferring equity interests and entering trademark license agreements. The reorganization completed in June 2022 is classified as a common control transaction, impacting the consolidated financial statements.

4. **Financial Performance**: The company reported significant net losses, with a net loss of $(1,104,925) thousand in 2024, reflecting a worsening trend from previous years. Operating expenses increased substantially, and investment income fluctuated. The total comprehensive loss also rose, indicating ongoing financial challenges.

5. **Cash Flow Dynamics**: The cash flow activities revealed a net cash outflow from operating activities, with substantial cash used in investing activities. Financing activities provided a notable increase in cash, but overall cash and restricted cash positions declined significantly by the end of 2024.

6. **Tax Considerations**: The company faces complexities related to income tax expenses, with significant fluctuations in current tax provisions across jurisdictions. The potential classification as a PRC resident enterprise raises concerns about tax liabilities.

7. **Valuation Allowances and Loss Carryforwards**: The company reported valuation allowances of $585,820 related to deferred tax assets, with net operating loss carryforwards totaling $840,064 available to offset future taxable profits.

8. **Related Party Transactions**: The company engaged in numerous transactions with related parties, including loans, purchases of vehicles and equipment, and lease agreements. These transactions reflect ongoing relationships with affiliated entities and their impact on financial statements.

9. **Future Outlook**: The company is exploring strategies to secure additional liquidity and navigate financial uncertainties, including potential equity financing and operational restructuring.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex financial landscape characterized by substantial share issuances, significant losses, and ongoing restructuring efforts, all while managing relationships with related parties and addressing tax implications. The company's future performance will depend on its ability to stabilize operations, enhance liquidity, and effectively manage its financial obligations.

title: Forward-Looking Statements 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Financial Performance for Lotus Technology Inc. (as of September 30, 2024)

This press release includes forward-looking statements as defined by the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, which are subject to risks and uncertainties outlined in the company's SEC filings. The information is current as of the release date, and Lotus Tech is not obligated to update these statements unless legally required.

#### Financial Overview

For the nine months ended September 30, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. reported significant financial changes compared to the same period in 2023:

- **Total Revenues:** Increased to $652,823,000 in 2024 from $317,941,000 in 2023.
  - **Sales Revenues:** Rose to $624,249,000 (2024) from $305,480,000 (2023).
  - **Service Revenues:** Increased to $28,574,000 (2024) from $12,461,000 (2023).
  
- **Total Costs of Revenues:** Increased to $593,708,000 in 2024 from $284,193,000 in 2023.
  - **Cost of Goods Sold:** Rose to $580,820,000 (2024) from $275,246,000 (2023).
  - **Cost of Services:** Increased to $12,888,000 (2024) from $8,947,000 (2023).
  
- **Gross Profit:** Increased to $59,115,000 in 2024 from $33,748,000 in 2023.

- **Total Operating Expenses:** Increased to $656,860,000 in 2024 from $541,624,000 in 2023.
  - **R&D Expenses:** Slightly decreased to $227,525,000 (2024) from $234,547,000 (2023).
  - **Selling and Marketing Expenses:** Increased to $259,804,000 (2024) from $191,231,000 (2023).
  - **General and Administrative Expenses:** Increased to $175,342,000 (2024) from $118,203,000 (2023).

- **Operating Loss:** Increased to $597,745,000 in 2024 from $507,876,000 in 2023.
- **Net Loss:** Increased to $666,520,000 in 2024 from $526,524,000 in 2023.
  - **Net Loss Attributable to Ordinary Shareholders:** Increased to $665,118,000 (2024) from $518,873,000 (2023).
  - **Loss per Ordinary Share:** $1.05 in 2024 compared to $1.10 in 2023.
  
- **Weighted Average Shares Outstanding:** Increased to 636,737,124 in 2024 from 474,621,603 in 2023.

#### Comprehensive Loss

- **Total Comprehensive Loss:** Reported at $(666,172,000) for 2024 compared to $(503,032,000) in 2023.
- **Total Comprehensive Loss Attributable to Ordinary Shareholders:** $(664,770,000) in 2024 versus $(495,445,000) in 2023.

#### Quarterly Performance (Three Months Ended September 30, 2024)

- **Total Revenues:** Increased to $254,708,000 in 2024 from $187,906,000 in 2023.
- **Net Loss:** Increased to $206,268,000 in 2024 from $173,582,000 in 2023.
- **Loss per Ordinary Share:** $0.30 in 2024 compared to $0.37 in 2023.
- **Total Comprehensive Loss:** $205,931,000 for 2024 compared to $183,094,000 in 2023.

#### Assets and Liabilities Overview (in thousands)

- **Total Assets:** Increased from $1,584,678 (Dec 31, 2023) to $2,587,224 (Sept 30, 2024).
  - **Current Assets:** Rose from $883,712 to $1,293,960, with cash decreasing from $418,941 to $191,300, while restricted cash increased significantly from $7,873 to $465,064.
  - **Non-Current Assets:** Increased from $700,966 to $1,293,264, including new loans receivable from related parties of $280,808.

- **Total Liabilities:** Rose from $2,411,850 (Dec 31, 2023) to $2,964,797 (Sept 30, 2024).
  - **Current Liabilities:** Increased from $1,757,281 to $2,129,955, with short-term borrowings from third parties rising from $226,772 to $703,130.
  - **Non-Current Liabilities:** Increased from $654,569 to $834,842, including new convertible notes due to related parties of $113,701.

#### Mezzanine Equity and Shareholders' Deficit (in thousands)

- **Series Pre-A and Series A Redeemable Convertible Preferred Shares:** Valued at $184,509 and $199,021 respectively as of Dec 31, 2023, with no corresponding values for Sept 30, 2024.
- **Total Shareholders' Deficit:** Increased from $(1,210,702) to $(377,573), with the accumulated deficit rising from $(1,588,773) to $(2,253,891).
- **Total Liabilities, Mezzanine Equity, and Shareholders' Deficit:** Totaled $2,587,224 as of Sept 30, 2024, compared to $1,584,678 at the end of 2023.

#### Non-GAAP Results

- **Adjusted Net Loss (Nine Months 2024):** $632,955,000.
- **Adjusted EBITDA (Nine Months 2024):** $(563,366,000).

This summary encapsulates the key financial metrics and results for Lotus Technology Inc. for the nine months and three months ended September 30, 2024, highlighting significant increases in revenues and losses, alongside a detailed breakdown of expenses and comprehensive losses. title: Financial Information 
summary: The financial analysis of Lotus Technology Inc. as of September 30, 2024, compared to December 31, 2023, reveals significant shifts in the company's financial position, encompassing its balance sheet, liabilities, mezzanine equity, shareholders' deficit, and comprehensive income.

**Balance Sheet Overview**: Total assets surged from $1,584,678 thousand at the end of 2023 to $2,587,224 thousand by September 2024. This increase was driven by substantial growth in cash equivalents, inventories, and both current and non-current assets, with notable contributions from new loan receivables and pledged securities. Cash and cash equivalents saw a sharp decline, while restricted cash rose dramatically.

**Liabilities and Equity**: Total liabilities rose significantly from $2,411,850 thousand to $2,964,797 thousand, with current liabilities accounting for a major portion of this increase. The shareholders' deficit improved markedly from $1,210,702 thousand to $377,573 thousand, largely due to a rise in additional paid-in capital. However, the accumulated deficit expanded, indicating ongoing financial challenges.

**Comprehensive Income Performance**: For the nine months ending September 30, 2024, the company reported a net loss of $666,520 thousand, an increase from the previous year's loss. Adjustments related to non-controlling interests and foreign currency translation were less favorable compared to 2023, leading to a total comprehensive loss that also increased year-over-year.

Overall, the financial data reflects Lotus Technology Inc.'s evolving landscape, influenced by strategic agreements and market dynamics, while highlighting both growth opportunities and persistent financial hurdles. All figures are presented in thousands of USD.
